course_change_2026: Full Loop 2026 — includes Rubicon section (miles of fine powder dust). 200.4 mi / 32,093 ft gain.
medical_form: Complete mandatory Medical Form before race — link on Gear page.
medical_leads: Annette Florczak, Tasha Swartley, Julie Burges (Tahoe 200 medical leads).
sleep_medical: Medical director: begin sleeping by Night 2 (within 24–40 h of start). Even 30–60 min helps. Severe delusion may mean a pull.
emergency_contact: Unless immediately life-threatening, contact race staff before 911. Rescue waits often 4–24 h.
mandatory_wag_bags: All human waste packed out. One bag provided at check-in. Pack 12-pack of extras in drop bags.
pacer_pickup: Pacers allowed beginning at Loon Lake (mile 87.6) and at any crew-access station after.
crew_vehicle: One vehicle per runner. Max 20 ft long × 8.5 ft wide. No RVs/trailers larger. Rivian R1S fits.
tahoe_city_parking: Crew parks at Transit Center, walks 0.2 mi to aid station.
brockway_parking: Crew parks in designated area, walks 0.6 mi to aid station.
sleep_limit: 6 hours max at sleep stations. Beds for runners first; pacers only if available.
wildlife: Sierra Nevada Yellow-legged Frog habitat — stay on trail. Bear activity reported in Homewood area.
medical_disqualification: IV during event = automatic DQ. EMS/SAR use = automatic DQ.
no_muling: Pacers may not carry runner's gear/food/water (except in emergency, which DQs the runner).
course_marking: Dragons (reflective ribbons) + reflective arrow signs. Weather and wildlife can disturb markers — GPX device is mandatory.
cell_service: Limited throughout course. Don't count on it for crew communication.
